Japanese: １２月２９日　午前１１時５４分 地方裁判所　被告人第５控え室
English: December 29, 11:54 AM District Court Defendant Lobby No. 5

Japanese: ハ、ハニイたち！
English: Sw-Sweetie!

Japanese: 言っておくが、ボクは 現場になんか行ってないぞ！
English: You have to believe me! I didn't go anywhere near that crime scene!

Japanese: じゃあ‥‥事件が 起こったころは‥‥？
English: So then where were you when the murder took place?

Japanese: きのうも話したろう。 団長室にいたんだよ！
English: We talked about it yesterday remember? I was in the Ringmaster's room!

Japanese: その途中で、団長は 出ていったんですよね。
English: And while you were there, it was the Ringmaster who left the room, right?

Japanese: そう。“ここで待っててくれ” って言ってね。
English: Exactly. He told me to wait in the room because he would "be right back".

Japanese: 団長さん‥‥そのとき、 現場に行ったのかな‥‥。
English: That's when the Ringmaster headed to the scene of the crime... Right?

Japanese: 当然、そうなるね。
English: That's what it seems like.

Japanese: でも‥‥団長さん、マックスの 衣装を着てたんだよね？
English: But... The Ringmaster must've been wearing Max's costume. Right?

Japanese: そうだハニイ！　思い出したよ！
English: Oh! Sweetie! I just remembered!

Japanese: ボク、ステージ衣装のまま 団長室に行ったんだ！
English: I went straight to the Ringmaster's room still dressed in my stage clothes.

Japanese: そして、そこで衣装を 脱いだんだよ！
English: But when I got there, I went ahead and took the costume off.

Japanese: ‥‥ってコトは？
English: Which means?

Japanese: 団長がそれを勝手に借りれば、 マックスに変装できた‥‥。
English: It means that the Ringmaster could have taken his costume, and went out looking like Max!

Japanese: ゴージャス！ じゅうぶん可能だろうね！
English: FABULOUS! That's a fabulously possible possibility!

Japanese: へええ。なるほどねえ‥‥。
English: Heh Heh... Well done Nick.

Japanese: でも‥‥ハニイ？
English: However, sweetie...

Japanese: どうして団長は、ぼくの 衣装なんか着たんだろう？
English: Why would the Ringmaster want to dress up like me? Isn't that a bit strange?
Hmm...

Japanese: それにさ。現場じゃあシルクハット しか見つからなかったんだろ。
English: If you think about it, all they found at the crime scene was my silk hat.

Japanese: マントは‥‥？ どこへ行ったんだい？
English: What about my cloak? Where did that go?
Double hmm...

Japanese: へええ。マックスさんて、 アタマもいいんですねー。
English: Wow Max. I never thought of that. You should be a detective or something!

Japanese: 小さいころは、魔術師になろうか 総理大臣になろうか‥‥、
English: Well, I was never quite sure what to be when I grew up... Magician or President?

Japanese: さんざんマヨったものさ！
English: You have no idea how hard it was to make a decision!

Japanese: カッコイイなあ‥‥。
English: That's really cool...

Japanese: （ゴージャス！ ナゾは深まるばかり、か‥‥）
English: (Fabulous... This mystery just keeps getting deeper...)

Japanese: 同日　午後１２時　６分 地方裁判所　第２法廷
English: December 29, 12:06 PM District Court Courtroom No. 2

Japanese: では、審理を再開しましょう。
English: Now that everyone is back, let's get started. The court is now back in session.

Japanese: ‥‥狩魔検事。 おねがいします。
English: Ms. von Karma, please proceed with the prosecution's case.

Japanese: ‥‥それでは、 証人を喚問するわ。
English: Very well... I will now call my next witness.

Japanese: 事件のすべてを目撃した あわれなピエロ、
English: A pitiful clown with the unfortunate distinction of having seen the entire thing.

Japanese: トミーを入廷させなさい！
English: Will Mr. Lawrence Curls please take the stand!

Japanese: （なんで“あわれな”ピエロ なんだ‥‥？）
English: (Why did she just call him a "pitiful" clown?)

Japanese: 証人。‥‥職業と名前を。
English: The witness will state his name and occupation for the court record.

Japanese: まぎゃらうぶろぼッ！
English: In West Clownadelphia, born and raised, on the playground is where I spent my clowin...

Japanese: ‥‥名前と職業。
English: Name and occupation.

Japanese: ど、どうしました？　証人。 どこか、具合でも‥‥
English: Will the witness please inform the court why he is speaking autobiographical gibberish?

Japanese: いや‥‥その‥‥
English: O-Oh... I-I'm sorry...

Japanese: こ、こういうところは 初めてなもので‥‥
English: I'm just not used to being in court. I've never been in a courtroom in my life.

Japanese: どういうギャグを 言えばいいのか‥‥と。
English: I wasn't quite sure what joke is best suited to this sort of occasion.

Japanese: なな、ナニを言ってるんですか！
English: What in the world are you talking about!?

Japanese: ここは裁きの庭です。 ユーモアなど、もっての他ですぞ！
English: You're in a grand hall of justice, not some comedy club!

Japanese: 職業はまあ‥‥、見ればおおむね わかるので、お名前を。
English: Since it's easy to see your occupation, please state your name for the court.

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥あ、そうだ！ こういうの、どうです？
English: Oh... Yeah... Maybe this joke is OK...

Japanese: “かあちゃん、パンツ破けた！” “マタかい？”‥‥なーんて。
English: "Mom, do I have to wear pants?" "The sign only says no shirt, no shoes, no service."

Japanese: あひゃ！　あひゃ。あひゃひゃ。 あひゃらあひゃらひゃら。
English: Aha! Aha! Aha! Aha! Aha! Aha! Aha! Aha! Aha! Aha!

Japanese: “このカレンダー、ダレのだぁ？” “カレんだぁ”‥‥とか！
English: OK! OK! How about this? "Have you met my proctologist? Dr. Seymour Butts!!"

Japanese: どう？　どう？　あひゃ！ あひゃひゃ！　あひゃひゃひゃ‥‥
English: How was that one!? Aha! Aha! Aha! Aha! Aha! Aha! Aha! Aha! Aha! Aha!

Japanese: あひゃらりるれほれらはりへほッ！
English: ...But a couple of clowns, they were up to no good... Started makin' trouble in...

Japanese: ‥‥名前を。
English: Your name...

Japanese: と、富田　松夫（とみたまつお）。 ‥‥ピエロやっとります。
English: Lawrence Curls. Professional funnyman, also known as Moe the clown.

Japanese: あの日の午後１０時１５分ごろ、 証人は事件を目撃した。
English: You witnessed the scene at around 10:15 PM the day of the murder.

Japanese: ‥‥まちがいないわね。
English: Correct?

Japanese: ‥‥ハイ。
English: Yes... Yes it is.

Japanese: では、証人。あなたが見たことを 証言してください。
English: Very well, Mr. Curls. Will you please testify to what you saw that evening?

Japanese: ガッテンしょーちの
English: A rabbi, a priest, and a Rastafarian walked into this plaza...

Japanese: ユーモアはヌキで。
English: Without the humor please.

Japanese: ‥‥ハイ。
English: OK...

Japanese: トミーさん、 なんかカワイソウ‥‥。
English: Aww... Poor Moe can't be his normal stoogey self in court.

Japanese: ～事件の当夜、目撃したこと～
English: -- What You Witnessed --

Japanese: わかってるんです。オレっちの ギャグセンス、イマイチだって。
English: I know... I know... I'm not the greatest comedian in the world...

Japanese: ここ１０年、客を心から笑わせた コトなんて、ありゃしない！
English: I haven't been able to make people laugh for 10 years!

Japanese: ナニを言っても、返ってくるのは ウツロなジャパニーズ・スマイル。
English: No matter what I say, all I get in return is a vacant stare and polite applause.

Japanese: ダレも笑わないから、自分で笑う のが、クセになっちまった。
English: Since no one ever laughs at my jokes, I've taken to laughing at them myself.

Japanese: でもね！　オレっちだって、 努力をしてないワケじゃない！
English: It's a bad habit, but hey, at least I'm trying!!

Japanese: 笑いのとれないピエロなんて！ オレっち‥‥いらない人間なのか？
English: Imagine my predicament! I'm a clown who can't make people laugh!! I'm almost useless!

Japanese: 今日だってそうだ！　とびっきりの ネタを用意してきたのに！
English: But I keep trying... I even tried to come up with jokes just for today!!

Japanese: ‥‥このフンイキにのまれて、 ナニも思い出せないときたもんだ！
English: But this atmosphere is very nerve-racking! I decided to try making everyone laugh!

Japanese: みなさん。どうなんですか！ オレっちは！
English: Seriously... Everyone... What do you think of me? How am I doing?

Japanese: ‥‥あの。コレに 尋問しなきゃいけないんですか？
English: Umm... Aren't we the ones supposed to be asking the questions here?

Japanese: ‥‥証人。
English: Witness...

Japanese: へ。
English: Huh?

Japanese: あなたの話は法廷が終わってから ゆっくり聞いてあげるから。
English: We will listen to your call for help after the court proceedings are over.

Japanese: だから、今は 事件の話をおねがい。
English: Thus, please stick with the facts of this case.

Japanese: ホントですか！ き、聞いてくれるんですか！
English: Really!? You'll really hear me out!?

Japanese: なんだったら、笑い役として 部下の刑事を呼んでもいいわ。
English: Well, I'll make sure that one of my staff will be your straight man later.

Japanese: わ、わかりました！ オレっち‥‥やります！
English: Thank you!! Thank you!! I can't wait!!

Japanese: （キノドクなイトノコ刑事‥‥）
English: (Poor Gumshoe...)

Japanese: では！　あらためて 証言してもらいましょう。
English: Now that that's settled, shall we begin once again with the testimony!?

Japanese: よーし！　しゃべるぞお！
English: Of course we can! I'll talk for as long as you want!!

Japanese: ～事件の当夜、目撃したこと～
English: -- What You Witnessed --

Japanese: 事件の夜は、練習が終わってすぐ、 宿舎の部屋にもどったんです。
English: The night of the murder, after practice was over, I went straight back to my room.

Japanese: その日はね。 スゴくつかれていたんですよ。
English: You have no idea how tired I was that night. I was pooped.

Japanese: 寝ようかと思って、フト なんの気なしに窓のそとを見ると。
English: I thought I'd go straight to sleep, but before I did, I glanced out the window.

Japanese: ２人の人影が見えたんです！ ちょっと、遠かったけど。
English: That's when I saw two silhouettes! They were a bit far away though!

Japanese: 団長と、マントをつけた マックスでした！
English: It was the Ringmaster, and he was with Max, who was wearing his cloak!

Japanese: 見ていると‥‥いきなり マックスが団長を殴ったんです！
English: I kept watching them, and all of a sudden, Max clonked the Ringmaster over the head!

Japanese: ‥‥ふ、ふむう‥‥
English: That's very interesting...

Japanese: その目撃にまちがいがなければ、 イキナリ決定的ですね‥‥。
English: If this eyewitness account is to be believed, I have enough to pass judgment right now...

Japanese: モチロン‥‥まちがいなど ありえないわ‥‥。
English: Of course you can! There is no way that this account can be criticized.

Japanese: ただ‥‥、 この証人はちょっとその。
English: However... The witness is a bit, how do you say, "off-kilter"...

Japanese: ‥‥ナニかやらかしそうな フンイキがありますからね。
English: Almost like he has some sort of atmosphere of guilt surrounding him.

Japanese: あひゃ。あひゃひゃ‥‥。 あ。これ、アイソ笑いですから。
English: Aha! Aha! Aha! That must be because of my insincere smile...

Japanese: 弁護人。 ‥‥尋問をおねがいします。
English: Mr. Wright, please begin the defense's cross-examination.

Japanese: はあ。
English: Yes, Your Honor.

Japanese: なるほどくん！　なんとしても ムジュンを見つけないと‥‥！
English: Nick! You've gotta find some kind of contradiction in his testimony!

Japanese: わかってるさ。
English: I know that!

Japanese: あ。弁護人。
English: Mr. Wright.

Japanese: はい。
English: Your Honor?

Japanese: この証人、ほっておくと法廷を ハメツさせるおそれがあります。
English: I'm afraid that if you push this witness too far, it would bring disaster upon the court.

Japanese: くれぐれも、ムダな ゆさぶりはひかえるように。
English: Thus, I sincerely hope you are not going to engage in pointless saber-rattling.

Japanese: は‥‥はあ。
English: I understand Your Honor.

Japanese: ‥‥よけいなツッコミには ペナルティを与えていただくわ。
English: If you cause this clown to stray from the facts, I'll hold you responsible!

Japanese: （‥‥ペナルティなら トミーさんにあげればいいのに）
English: (Why am I responsible!? I'm not the one with the corny jokes.)

Japanese: ～事件の当夜、目撃したこと～
English: -- What You Witnessed --

Japanese: 事件の夜は、練習が終わってすぐ、 宿舎の部屋にもどったんです。
English: The night of the murder, after practice was over, I went straight back to my room.

Japanese: その日はね。 スゴくつかれていたんですよ。
English: You have no idea how tired I was that night. I was pooped.

Japanese: ２人の人影が見えたんです！ ちょっと、遠かったけど。
English: That's when I saw two silhouettes! They were a bit far away though!

Japanese: 団長と、マントをつけた マックスでした！
English: It was the Ringmaster, and he was with Max, who was wearing his cloak!

Japanese: 見ていると‥‥いきなり マックスが団長を殴ったんです！
English: I kept watching them and, all of a sudden, Max clonked the Ringmaster over the head!

Japanese: なんか、トミーさんの話‥‥。 きのうと、ちょっとちがうような。
English: You know... Moe's testimony and what he told us yesterday are kinda different...

Japanese: うーん‥‥。そこを ピンポイントでゆさぶってみるか。
English: Yeah I noticed. We've just got to pinpoint what's changed.

Japanese: がんばって！
English: You can do it, Nick!

Japanese: 寝ようかと思って、フト なんの気なしに窓のそとを見ると。
English: I thought I'd go straight to sleep, but before I did, I glanced out the window.

Japanese: 窓のそとで大きな音がしたんで、 そっちを見たんですよ‥‥。
English: I heard a huge noise outside the window, and that's what made me take a look outside...

Japanese: 練習が終わったのは、 たしか１０時でしたね？
English: You'd say that practice wrapped up around 10 PM correct?

Japanese: モンダイはそれなんだよ。
English: See, that's the thing...
Huh?

Japanese: “１０時”をネタにした ギャグがどうも浮かばなくてさ。 10:00 PM?
English: How exactly am I supposed to make a joke about

Japanese: これが“８時”だったら、 バカウケだったんだけどさあ。
English: Now eight o'clock... That's something a clown can riff off of...

Japanese: ‥‥聞く？聞く？せっかくだし、 ８時だったことにしてさ。
English: Let's just pretend it was eight o'clock... That way I can make a great joke!

Japanese: や、やめておきます。
English: Let's not make it 8:00 PM, alright Moe?

Japanese: なんだよそれ！ ピエロをナメんなよな！
English: What do you mean let's not! Don't take the clown out of the clowning around!

Japanese: 聞いておこうよ、なるほどくん！
English: C'mon Nick, just listen to his joke!

Japanese: ‥‥モンダイが、３つある。 １つ目。たぶん笑えない。
English: There are three reasons why I don't want to... One, I probably won't laugh.

Japanese: ２つ目。ペナルティを食らう。 ３つ目。ムチも食らう。
English: Two, I'll get hit with a penalty. Three, I'll get whipped.

Japanese: ‥‥証人。 先をつづけてください。
English: Will the witness just proceed with his factual testimony...

Japanese: つまんないの。
English: You are sooo boring.

Japanese: やっぱり。聞きたいでしょ。 ‥‥じゃ、シツレイして。
English: You know you want to hear the joke... A man goes to his doctor and the doctor asks,

Japanese: “かあちゃん、オレの
English: "How is number 1?" The man says "Everyday at six." The doctor asks, "And 2?"

Japanese: まらばらげらばらめらみらぶらべら
English: The man says "Everyday at seven." "Then what's the matter?" "I slept til eight."

Japanese: ‥‥それは、あとでウチの 部下にタップリ聞かせてあげて。
English: Witness! These are exactly the type of jokes you should save for Gumshoe!

Japanese: いたたッ！
English: OWWW!!

Japanese: あなたも 聞きたそうなカオ、しない！
English: You knew what would happen! Don't look at me like that!

Japanese: ‥‥弁護人。ヤクソクどおり、 ペナルティを与えます。
English: Mr. Wright, you're hereby penalized! This will not become a kangaroo court!

Japanese: （く、クルシイ戦いだ ‥‥いろんなイミで‥‥）
English: (This isn't funny at all... In more ways that one...)

Japanese: “つかれていた”‥‥？
English: You were "pooped" that night?

Japanese: そう。もう、つかれきってたね。 ‥‥オシッコしすぎちゃって。
English: Yes. I was very very tired... Not to mention I had some bad mexican food.

Japanese: は？
English: What?

Japanese: “小水しきったカオ” ‥‥ちょっとムズカシかったか？
English: The double cheese double bean enchilada is a dangerous menu item...

Japanese: あひゃ！　あひゃひゃあひゃ。 あっひゃひゃあひゃひゃあひゃ。
English: What happened after that, as they say, "happens"... Aha! Aha! Aha! Aha! Aha! Aha!

Japanese: ‥‥どうよ？　今のは クールなギャグだろ？　な？
English: That one was cool, huh? I hear the kids really love toilet humor these days...

Japanese: ‥‥クールすぎて しずかになっちゃいましたね。
English: That joke was so hip, I don't want to ruin it with a reply.

Japanese: ‥‥弁護人、笑えません。 ペナルティを与えます。
English: I've got a good reply though. That joke was so funny it just earned you a penalty!

Japanese: フト窓のそとを見た‥‥？
English: You just happened to glance out of the window?

Japanese: モチロン！　“ハト”でも “ホト”でもなく、“フト”ね！
English: You could say that. You could also say I peeked, stared, glimpsed, peeped, eyeballed...

Japanese: ‥‥証人ッ！
English: Mr. Curls!!

Japanese: うう‥‥これもダメか‥‥
English: Oh... I guess synonyms aren't allowed either...

Japanese: （どうする‥‥？　もう一歩、 踏みこんでみるか‥‥）
English: (What should I do? I wonder if I should press him further on this issue...)

Japanese: じゃ、証人。 証言にもどってください。
English: Very well, Moe. How about we get back to your testimony now?

Japanese: ‥‥今回の尋問は タンパクですね、弁護人。
English: This is definitely one of your more straightforward cross- examinations, Mr. Wright.

Japanese: （ペナルティを食らうワケには いかないからな‥‥）
English: (Of course! You threatened me with a penalty if I don't keep this stooge in check!)

Japanese: ‥‥なぜ、窓のそとを 見たんですか？
English: Exactly why did you look out of your window that night?

Japanese: な、なんだよ！
English: Why!? Why!?

Japanese: ピエロは、理由がなくちゃ 見ちゃイカンの？　窓のそと。
English: Clowns don't need a reason to look out their windows, do they!?

Japanese: そうじゃありません。
English: That's not what I meant.

Japanese: ただ‥‥きのうのお話では、 たしか‥‥
English: I meant that... Well... When we spoke yesterday...

Japanese: フトンに入ったとたん、 ものすごい音がしたんだ。
English: Once I had tucked myself into bed, I heard this amazing noise. It was incredibly loud.

Japanese: “ドカッ！”‥‥って。
English: It sounded like a giant "THUMP"!

Japanese: ああ、はいはい！ ‥‥アレね！
English: Oh yeah! I forgot about that!!
You... Forgot!?

Japanese: 裁判長。この証人は、 大きな音がしたから窓を見た。
English: Your Honor, the witness looked out of his window upon hearing a loud sound.

Japanese: “なんの気なしに”見た ワケではなかったのです！
English: He did not just simply "glance" out of his window that night.

Japanese: そーだよ！　ドカッ、て！ 言わなかった？　オレっち。
English: Oh yeah! That's right! I forgot to mention that "THUMP" didn't I?

Japanese: はわはわはわはわはわはわはわはわ
English: Owwy Oww Oww...

Japanese: ゼンゼン言ってないッ！
English: That's not something you just "forget to mention"!

Japanese: ‥‥まあ、とにかく そういうワケなので‥‥
English: Umm... Yeah... What she said...

Japanese: 証言を修正してください！
English: I believe it would be best if Moe were to revise his testimony.

Japanese: ‥‥ふむう‥‥
English: Hmm...

Japanese: わかりました。 証人。証言を修正してください。
English: Very well. Mr. Curls. Please revise your testimony.

Japanese: （これで‥‥ 状況は変わるはずだ！）
English: (This should start turning the tables in our favor!)

Japanese: 遠かった‥‥。 どれぐらいのキョリが？
English: Far away you say... If you had to say exactly, how far away were they?

Japanese: そうだなあ。
English: Let me think about that for a second...

Japanese: ‥‥ここ、オレっちの部屋。
English: If my room is here...

Japanese: ‥‥２人はこの辺にいたから‥‥。 ま、１０メートルぐらいかね。
English: And they looked about yay- big... I'd say they were about 30 feet from my window.

Japanese: １０メートル‥‥そう遠い キョリではないわね。
English: Just 30 feet? That's not far at all!

Japanese: あの晩は、雪がやんで、 少しキリが出ていたけどなあ‥‥
English: It was snowing that night, and it cut down on visibility.

Japanese: わかりました。では、その ２つの人影について証言を。
English: I see. Please continue with your testimony regarding the two shadows that you saw.

Japanese: ハッキリ見えたんですか？ “ちょっと遠かった”そうですが。
English: You'd say you clearly saw this? Even though you were, by your own admission, far away?

Japanese: ううーん。そうだなあ。 あれから何度も考えたし、
English: That's right. I had been thinking about it over and over since that night,

Japanese: そこのカルマ検事さんとも 打ち合わせして思い出したんです。
English: but things didn't really make sense until I spoke with the prosecutor, Ms. von Karma.

Japanese: ‥‥たしかに、マックスと 団長を見たはずです、オレっち。
English: But now I am 100% certain that it was Max and the Ringmaster that I saw that night.

Japanese: ホレ。アイツ、 キザなシンボルがあるでしょ？
English: Just think about it? How could I be wrong if Max is always wearing his uppity symbols?

Japanese: ‥‥キザなシンボル？
English: Uppity symbols?

Japanese: 最近のベンゴシさんは なんにも知らないねぇ。
English: Lawyers nowadays!! Do you even have to go to school anymore to be one?

Japanese: はい！　じゃあみなさん、 トミーとごいっしょに！
English: Alright! Everyone knows what to do!! Altogether now!! Say it with Uncle Moe!

Japanese: いっつもコレだ。 客がついてきたタメシがない。
English: See what I mean!! It's always like this! The crowd never wants to go along with me!

Japanese: ‥‥やっぱりオレっち、 ピエロとしてシッカク‥‥
English: I must really be utterly and completely worthless as a clown...

Japanese: あらばまみねそたねばだばあじにあ
English: Yeowza!

Japanese: ‥‥キホン的にクドい。
English: Enough foolishness, get back on track!

Japanese: 証人。見たことだけを 話してください。
English: Will the witness please testify as to what he saw... And only what he saw!

Japanese: 殴る瞬間を見たわけですか？
English: You say you saw the Ringmaster get "clonked" over the head!?

Japanese: そりゃそうでしょう！ クライマックスだし。
English: Yes I did!! It's the climax of my story!

Japanese: （なんだか、よくわからないコト 言ってるけど‥‥）
English: (He really does enjoy the completely random non sequitur...)

Japanese: 被害者をなぐった凶器は なんでしたか？
English: What would you say the victim was struck with?

Japanese: キョーキ？ そんなの知りませんよ。
English: You mean weapon? I have no idea.

Japanese: だって、現場から 見つかってないんでしょ？
English: A weapon wasn't found at the murder scene, right?

Japanese: いやいやいや。だって、 目撃してたんですよね‥‥？
English: No no no... You did say you saw the entire thing, didn't you?

Japanese: あ、あれ。そうか。 うーん‥‥そうねえ‥‥。
English: Well... I, umm... Yeah, I suppose I did.

Japanese: ダメだわ。凶器、見てないや。 あひゃ。あひゃ。あひゃ。
English: Wait... No I didn't. I didn't see a weapon... Heh heh heh!

Japanese: トミーさん！
English: Moe!

Japanese: あなた、ホントに見たんですか？ 犯行の瞬間を‥‥
English: Did you or did you not see the crime of murder committed that night!?

Japanese: ムダなゆさぶりは認めない ‥‥そう言ったハズよ。
English: I will not permit you to harass my witness in this manner!

Japanese: この証人を攻撃するなら、 それなりのコンキョがあるはず。
English: You'd better have an excellent reason for attacking this poor, poor clown.

Japanese: もしないのならば‥‥ ペナルティはこれぐらいね。
English: Because if you don't... You know what is waiting for you... A nice penalty!

Japanese: ぐ‥‥ （ちょっと、大きすぎないか？）
English: *gulp* (Wasn't that a bit melodramatic...)

Japanese: さあ、どうかしら？ 成歩堂　龍一！
English: So what will it be then, Mr. Phoenix Wright?

Japanese: 証人が、犯行の瞬間を見ていない という、明確なコンキョはあるの？
English: Do you have any clear basis to believe that my witness did not see the crime?

Japanese: ‥‥ま。今のところ、 コンキョはありませんケド。
English: Well... See... Right now, I don't have a clear basis to be thinking that...

Japanese: ということは、これは “イミのないゆさぶり”‥‥。
English: Then this is just another one of your pointless efforts to badger my witness!

Japanese: 私からペナルティを 与えるわ！
English: Thus, you have just earned yourself a penalty... FROM ME!!

Japanese: ま、まあまあ狩魔検事。 お待ちなさい。
English: Now wait a second Ms. von Karma!

Japanese: 何も、そこまでキビしく しなくてもいいでしょう。
English: This time, I do not feel the need to treat Mr. Wright so harshly.

Japanese: そ、そうですよ！ （やれやれ。助かったか‥‥）
English: That's right! What he said! (Bailed out by the judge! What an honor indeed!)

Japanese: 今の発言のペナルティなら、 せいぜいこのぐらいです。
English: If we must penalize Mr. Wright for his display, I think a verbal censure would be best.

Japanese: え。
English: Huh?

Japanese: いけませんよ、弁護人。 コンキョもなくゆさぶっては。
English: You know the rules Mr. Wright. There is no room for baseless hounding in this courtroom.

Japanese: （ケッキョク 食らってしまった‥‥）
English: (I guess he didn't really bail me out after all...)

Japanese: コンキョはあります！
English: Of course I have grounds to make this claim!

Japanese: 聞かせてもらおうかしら。
English: Then let's hear it!

Japanese: 今はまだ、言えません。
English: That's something I can't share with you at this time.

Japanese: なな、ナニ言ってるんですか！ 弁護人！
English: W-W-What did you just say!? Mr. Wright!!

Japanese: （証言が、なんとなくヘンなのは わかってるんだけど‥‥）
English: (I know there's something wrong with this testimony...)

Japanese: （ハッキリしたコンキョが ないんだよな‥‥）
English: (But I can't put why I feel this way into words for the Judge...)

Japanese: ねえ。もう少し情報を 集めてみようよ。
English: Maybe we should get a bit more information...

Japanese: 何かコンキョが見つかったら、 またチャレンジしよう！
English: Once we find something more solid, we can give it another shot!

Japanese: ‥‥わかった、そうだね。
English: Yeah, you're right...

Japanese: じゃあ、証言にもどる前に、 ヤクソクどおり、ペナルティを。
English: Mr. Wright, before we resume testimony, you know that you must be penalized.

Japanese: （‥‥これはイタいな‥‥）
English: (Yikes. That's harsh...)

Japanese: （ヘタにゆさぶって ケガするのもイヤだしな）
English: (I don't feel like getting trampled on because I push Moe the wrong way.)

Japanese: トミーさん、ビミョーなコト 言ってるよねー。
English: Moe sure does seem to be making some questionable statements, don't you think?

Japanese: ‥‥でも、こいつをゆさぶる前に、 何か確証がほしいな。
English: I just wish there was some way we could get proof before I push him too far...

Japanese: うーん‥‥。
English: I know.

Japanese: どんな音でしたか？
English: What was the sound like?

Japanese: そうだなあ‥‥ええと‥‥ なんと言えばイイのか‥‥
English: Well... I guess it kinda sounded like... Hmm... I guess you could say...

Japanese: 証人。 ‥‥ユーモアはいりませんよ。
English: Mr. Curls. May the court remind you that humor is unnecessary.

Japanese: アレ。バレちゃいました？ あひゃっひゃ。ひゃ。
English: Doh! How'd you know that I was gonna make a joke! Aha! Aha! Aha! Aha! Aha!

Japanese: あのときの“音”ね‥‥
English: I guess that the sound sounded like a...

Japanese: ‥‥そう。何かカタいもので、 思いっきり人を殴ったような‥‥
English: I suppose it sounded like someone getting hit with something very hard...

Japanese: ま。そんな音でしたね。 マジなハナシ。
English: Yep. That's what it sounded like. Honestly.

Japanese: “人を殴ったような‥‥”
English: "Someone getting hit..." huh?

Japanese: ‥‥それで？　窓のそとを 見ると‥‥？
English: What then? You went to look out the window and you saw...

Japanese: コンキョはあります！
English: I've got a great reason to make my claim!

Japanese: 聞かせてもらおうかしら。
English: And I suppose you will be telling us all that "great" reason?

Japanese: ‥‥もちろん、そのコンキョは‥‥
English: Of course I will... The reason is...

Japanese: 証人の証言ですよ！
English: The witness' very own testimony!

Japanese: ‥‥ど、どういうことですか、 弁護人？
English: What is the meaning of that Mr. Wright?

Japanese: トミーさんは、“ドカッ”という、 人が殴られた音を聞いています。
English: Moe said that he heard a sound like a "thump" of someone getting hit.

Japanese: ふむう‥‥そうですね。
English: Hmm... He did say that.

Japanese: しかし、トミーさんはさっき、 こんな証言をしました。
English: However, Moe just stated the following under oath.

Japanese: 見ていると‥‥いきなり マックスが団長を殴ったんです！
English: I kept watching them, and all of a sudden, Max clonked the Ringmaster over the head!

Japanese: ‥‥殴られた音を聞いてから 窓のほうを見たのならば！
English: If Moe is to be believed when he says he looked out the window upon hearing a sound,

Japanese: 犯行の瞬間など 目撃できるはずがない！
English: there is no way that he could have seen Max "clonk" anyone!

Japanese: いなれしもかりおとのそにかしたッ
English: In 1972, a crack clown unit was sent to prison by a circus court for a crime...

Japanese: どうなんですか！　証人！
English: Mr. Curls! How do you respond to Mr. Wright's assessment!!?

Japanese: え‥‥ええと‥‥ ちょ、ちょっと考え中。
English: ...they didn't commit. These clowns promptly escaped from a maximum security clown car...

Japanese: ‥‥ユーモアはいりませんよ。
English: Mr. Curls, are you reciting the C-Team theme to anger this court?

Japanese: ちゃ‥‥ちゃんと 思い出してるんですよ‥‥
English: No no no... I'm just stalling for time while I jog my memory...

Japanese: やったね！　なるほどくん！
English: Great job, Nick!

Japanese: ‥‥ああいうタイプの証人は、 キオクを修正しちゃうんだよ。
English: These types of witnesses always seem to have a selective memory...

Japanese: 自分が目立ちたいからね。 ‥‥悪気はないんだけど。
English: You just have to peel back the layers of the clown make-up to find the truth.

Japanese: あのお‥‥。
English: Well... Umm...

Japanese: 思い出しましたか？
English: Ahh, you're back from your jog?

Japanese: はあ。‥‥やっぱり、最初に 思ったとおりでした。
English: Well... It pretty much happened the way I said.
"Pretty much"...?

Japanese: オレっちが窓を見たときは‥‥ 団長、もう倒れていたんです。
English: When I looked out my window that night, the Ringmaster was already face down in the snow.

Japanese: 検事さんと打ち合わせしてるうち、 つい、思いこんじまって‥‥
English: The prosecution helped me fill in the gaps of my statement.

Japanese: （か‥‥狩魔検事！）
English: (Von... Von Karma!!)

Japanese: （また、証言の操作を‥‥！）
English: (Tampering with witnesses again!!)

Japanese: ‥‥では証人は、被告が殴った 瞬間を見ていないワケですね？
English: So now you are saying that you did not see the defendant "clonk" the Ringmaster?

Japanese: は、はい‥‥。
English: Y-Yes...

Japanese: オレっちが窓を見たときにはもう、 アフター・カーニバルで。
English: When I looked out my window, the Ringmaster had already checked out.

Japanese: あふたー・かーにばる‥‥？
English: Checked out?

Japanese: “あとのマツリ”ってコト‥‥。 あひゃ。あひゃひゃ。あひゃあひゃ
English: Yep. He was on permanent vacation as they say. Aha! Aha! Aha! Aha! Aha!

Japanese: 証人！
English: Mr. Curls!!

Japanese: は‥‥はいッ。
English: Your Honor?

Japanese: あなたは犯行の瞬間を見ていない。 しかし、犯人は見ているのですね？
English: You did not witness the actual crime; however, you still say you saw the criminal, correct?

Japanese: そそそ、そうです！
English: Y-Y-YES!! Exactly!

Japanese: うずくまる団長のそばに、 人影が見えましたッ！
English: The Ringmaster was slumped over and I saw someone's silhouette next to him.

Japanese: では、その“人影”について 証言してください。‥‥ただし！
English: Very well. Then please testify to this silhouette you saw... I expect the truth!

Japanese: また、ユーモラスなことを 言おうとした場合は‥‥、
English: And if I even catch a hint of a joke from you...

Japanese: 私が個人的に、なんらかの罪を 着せます。‥‥気をつけるように。
English: I will put you in a holding cell until you learn court etiquette, got it!?

Japanese: ‥‥ハイ。
English: Got it.

Japanese: ～目撃した“人影”について～
English: -- The Silhouette --

Japanese: ちょっとキョリはあったけど、 あれはたしかに、マックスでした！
English: I was a bit far away, but that shadow could only have belonged to Max.

Japanese: だって、キザなシンボルがハッキリ 見えましたからね。
English: There's no doubting it. Especially since I saw his uppity symbols.

Japanese: シルクハットに、黒いマント。 ゼンブそろってるだろ、シンボル。
English: His silk hat. That black cloak. They were all there!

Japanese: カオのシルエットもね。 まちがいなくあの男でしたよ。
English: His face was silhouetted, but there was no doubt that it was him.

Japanese: マントがヒラヒラしてたから、 何を持ってるか見えませんでした。
English: His cloak was fluttering in the wind, so I couldn't really see what he was carrying.

Japanese: ふむう‥‥たしかに、現場にいた のは、被告人だったようですね。
English: Hmm... It does seem as if the defendant was at the scene of the crime.

Japanese: ちょっとゴタゴタしたけど‥‥、
English: You took your time realizing that, didn't you!?

Japanese: これで、すべての問題は なくなったのではなくて‥‥？
English: Whatever. That should be enough, right!?

Japanese: ‥‥たしかに。 決定的ですね、これで‥‥。
English: It is decisive testimony...

Japanese: マックス‥‥ 現場に行ったのかな‥‥？
English: Was Max really at the crime scene that night...?

Japanese: 本人は行ってないと言ってる。 ‥‥それを信じるしかない。
English: He said he wasn't there. We have to keep believing that.

Japanese: それでは、弁護人。 ‥‥尋問を、おねがいします。
English: Alright, Mr. Wright. Commence your cross- examination.

Japanese: ～目撃した“人影”について～
English: -- The Silhouette --

Japanese: ちょっとキョリはあったけど、 あれはたしかに、マックスでした！
English: I was a bit far away, but that shadow could only have belonged to Max.

Japanese: だって、キザなシンボルがハッキリ 見えましたからね。
English: There's no doubting it. Especially since I saw his uppity symbols.

Japanese: シルクハットに、黒いマント。 ゼンブそろってるだろ、シンボル。
English: His silk hat. That black cloak. They were all there!

Japanese: カオのシルエットもね。 まちがいなくあの男でしたよ。
English: His face was silhouetted, but there was no doubt that it was him.

Japanese: マントがヒラヒラしてたから、 何を持ってるか見えませんでした。
English: His cloak was fluttering in the wind, so I couldn't really see what he was carrying.

Japanese: （‥‥リロくんの証言と この証言があれば‥‥）
English: (With Trilo's testimony, and now Moe's...)

Japanese: （まちがいなく、 マックスは有罪だ！）
English: (It really seems like Max is the killer.)

Japanese: と、とにかく、ムジュンを 探さないとね！
English: N-Nick... You've gotta find a contradiction in this.

Japanese: ‥‥それだけじゃ足りないよ。
English: That's not going to be enough...
What?

Japanese: “トミーさんが目撃したのは マックスじゃなかった”‥‥
English: I have to prove that Moe saw someone other than Max that night!

Japanese: それを証明するんだ！ ‥‥なんとしても、ね！
English: That's the only thing that will help! I've gotta do it!

Japanese: リロくんといい、あなたといい、 カンタンに断言しすぎなのでは？
English: Don't you think that you are going a little overboard with how explicit you're being?

Japanese: 『まちがいなくマックスだった』 ‥‥と。
English: 'That shadow belonged to Max' is an awfully firm statement, don't you think?

Japanese: そ、そんなコト言われても‥‥
English: What are you getting at...?

Japanese: サーカスの仲間のイノチが かかってるんですよ。
English: I'm just saying that one of your fellow performer's life is on the line here.

Japanese: ‥‥それでもやっぱり、 マックスだと断言できますか？
English: Are you truly, 100% absolutely certain that it was Max?

Japanese: オレっち、そこまで ヒドいニンゲンじゃないよ。
English: I'm not the kind of person who would lie about something as serious as this!

Japanese: ‥‥人影はマックスだった。 オレっちはそうカクシンしてる。
English: The silhouette I saw was Max. I'm convinced of that.

Japanese: （‥‥どうやら、今度は 早トチリではなさそうだな‥‥）
English: (It doesn't seem like he is jumping to any conclusions this time...)

Japanese: じゃあ、うかがいましょう。 マックスだと確信した理由は？
English: Let's go over this again then. What makes you so sure that you saw Max that night?

Japanese: ‥‥リロくんもそう言ってました。
English: Trilo said the same thing.

Japanese: でも、シンボルだけなら、ぼくでも 身につけることができますよ？
English: But if it was just the symbols, then even I could have been wearing them.

Japanese: だれかがマックスに 変装していた、ということは？
English: What if someone had just taken Max's symbols and worn them?

Japanese: オレっちだって、それぐらい 考えたって。
English: I thought of that myself.

Japanese: でも、カオのリンカクもね。 どう見てもマックスだったよ。
English: But just looking at the shape of the face I saw, it must have been Max.

Japanese: ふ、ふむう‥‥ そうだったんですか！
English: Hmm... You're sure of that!?

Japanese: ‥‥バカなオトコ‥‥
English: Such a silly little boy...

Japanese: みずからワナの口を閉ざして くれるとはね。
English: He threw his entire case headlong into a trap!

Japanese: まちがいなく、 目撃したんですね？
English: You're sure that is what you saw that night?

Japanese: オレっち、見たことはちゃんと、 話してるって。バッチリ。
English: Exactly what I said I saw is exactly what I saw.

Japanese: オレっちね。タカのような目を してるんだからさあ！
English: I've got eyes like a hawk!

Japanese: （‥‥トリって、夜は目が 見えないんじゃなかったっけ）
English: (Umm... Don't birds have terrible night vision?)

Japanese: ‥‥しかも、 それだけじゃないんだ！
English: But that's not all I saw!

Japanese: ‥‥部屋の窓から、そんなに 細かいトコロまで見えたんですか？
English: You were able to see that kind of detail from your window?

Japanese: 現場付近には明かりがあった。
English: There was a light near the scene of the crime.

Japanese: ‥‥ふつうの視力があれば、 問題なく見えるはず。
English: Anyone with decent eyesight should have been able to see that much.

Japanese: ちなみに、視力はいくつですか？
English: Out of curiosity Moe, what is your eyesight like?

Japanese: 右６２キロ、左６０キロ。 なーんて、これはアクリョク‥‥
English: I can see like a hawk out of my left eye, and an eagle out of my right eye...

Japanese: ‥‥資料によれば、両方とも １．２、ね。
English: Our records say his eyesight is a perfect 20/20.

Japanese: （じゅうぶん見えた、という ことになるな‥‥）
English: (That must mean he could have seen the crime scene clearly.)

Japanese: ふむう‥‥聞けば聞くほどに アヤしくなっていきますねえ。
English: Hmm... The more we look into this matter, the more suspicious it becomes.

Japanese: ‥‥やっぱり、“アクリョク”は なかったかなあ‥‥
English: Hey! You asked me what my eyesight was like, not what it actually was!

Japanese: 『犯人は何か持っていた』‥‥と 考えているんですか？
English: So, you didn't actually see what the criminal was holding?

Japanese: だってさ。すごい音だった ワケよ。殴られたとき。
English: That's correct. I've been curious to know what caused that loud noise...

Japanese: ありゃあ、なんかカターいモンで 思いっきりやったんだ。
English: It must have been something incredibly hard.

Japanese: （今のところ、凶器は発見 されてないんだよな‥‥）
English: (Too bad they haven't found the murder weapon to know for sure...)

Japanese: “平たい鈍器”って書いてあるね。 ‥‥解剖記録には。
English: They claim it was a blunt object... At least it says so in the autopsy report.

Japanese: マックスのキザなシンボルを、 すべて見た‥‥？
English: You say you saw all of Max's "uppity" symbols?

Japanese: そうですよ？ シルクハットと、マント。
English: I suppose so... The silk hat and the cloak, right?

Japanese: ‥‥トミーさん。
English: Moe...

Japanese: マックス・ギャラクティカのキザな シンボルは‥‥３つでしたよね。
English: Everyone knows that Maximillion Galactica has three uppity symbols!

Japanese: みっつ‥‥？
English: Three symbols?

Japanese: さあ、みなさんごいっしょに！
English: Yay! Everyone get ready!! All... Together... Now...

Japanese: “シルクハット！　マント！ ‥‥白いバラ！”
English: "SILK HAT! CLOAK! WHITE ROSES!"

Japanese: ぐぎゃらあッ！
English: What the!?

Japanese: ‥‥何かと思えば、そんなコト。
English: Who cares if he knew that there were three or not!

Japanese: トーゼン、証人は見てるわ。 ただ、言い忘れただけ。
English: He saw what he saw. And he saw the symbols. He just forgot to mention one.

Japanese: そ、そうでしょ？　証人。
English: Isn't that right, Moe?
Do you like Pi? I love Pi!

Japanese: ‥‥聞かれたら、とりあえず “はい”でしょッ！
English: Silence fool! You are to respond with the whole truth! No fractions!

Japanese: そこまで！　‥‥証人の目は、 アキラカに泳いでいます！
English: Order! Order!

Japanese: トミーさん！ バラは見ていないようですね？
English: Moe! You didn't see the roses, did you!?

Japanese: ‥‥正直なトコロ‥‥
English: To be honest...

Japanese: アイツのムネに、バラなんか ついてなかったケドなあ‥‥
English: There wasn't any roses on the person I saw...

Japanese: 現場は暗かった！ 見落としただけにすぎない！
English: The crime scene was dark! It's obvious it was too dark to see that kind of detail!

Japanese: しかし、証人は人影の リンカクまで見わけている！
English: But the witness said he was able to see the silhouette of the criminal's face.

Japanese: しかも、バラは白かった！ 見落とすはずがない！
English: Not to mention that the roses are white! There's no way he could have missed them!

Japanese: ムネのバラなど、被害者と争って いるうちに、落ちてしまったのよ！
English: Then the roses must have fallen off when the defendant assaulted the victim!

Japanese: それならば、現場付近で 白いバラが発見されるはずです！
English: If that was the case, then the police would have found them near the crime scene!

Japanese: 弁護人！　ムネの白いバラが、 そんなに重要なのですか？
English: Mr. Wright! Are these white roses truly material to the facts of this case?

Japanese: とんでもない！ ただのワルあがきよ！
English: Clearly not! He is just toying with this court!

Japanese: （たしかに、ササイなことだ‥‥）
English: (I got her on the ropes now...)

Japanese: （でも、いつも、真実はササイな ほころびからカオを出してきた）
English: (Whenever there's a nugget of truth, it always seems to peek out at me... Then run away.)

Japanese: ‥‥いつだって、そうだった！
English: Someone is toying with the court, but it's not me!

Japanese: 裁判長！　リロくんの証言を 思い出してください。
English: Your Honor! Do you recall Trilo's testimony?

Japanese: キザなシンボル３点セットを、 オレはまちがいなく見たしな！
English: There's no way I could mistake someone wearing those three ridiculous symbols!

Japanese: イヤミな３点セットをぶら下げて、 鼻先を通りすぎやがったんだ！
English: How can you mistake someone with that crazy get-up and his nose stuck up so high...

Japanese: あのキザったらしい３点セット、 見まちがえようがねえよ！
English: How could you mistake someone wearing such a snobby 3 piece get-up!

Japanese: リロくんは目撃している！ マックスの、３つめのシンボルを！
English: Trilo saw them all! Trilo saw all three of Max's symbols!

Japanese: しかしこの証人は、 バラはなかったと言う！
English: However, this witness claims there were no white roses on the person he saw!

Japanese: これはあきらかに、 ムジュンしています！
English: There is absolutely no doubt that this is a contradiction!

Japanese: ‥‥ふむう‥‥これは どう考えるべきなのでしょう？
English: Hmm... Now what am I supposed to think?

Japanese: たしかに、取るに足らない、 ササイなコトに思えます。
English: One is supposed to disregard the pointless, but this...

Japanese: 裁判長！　白いバラは忘れて 他の証言を思い出しなさい！
English: Judge! Forget the roses! Think about his other testimony!

Japanese: 証人が見たのは、アキラカに マックス・ギャラクティカよ！
English: The witness has stated without a doubt that he saw Maximillion Galactica!

Japanese: これ以上の審理はムダ！ ‥‥終わりにするの。
English: Nothing else matters! Let's wrap this case up now!

Japanese: 裁判長！　どんなに小さくても、 ギモンは残すべきじゃない！
English: Your Honor! It may be trivial, but there should still be doubt lingering in your mind!

Japanese: 正直なところ、私は この証人自身にギモンを感じます。
English: Frankly, I have my doubts about this witness.

Japanese: トシの割に、落ちつきがない。
English: It seems that, unlike wine, the witness has not grown more mature with age.

Japanese: うう‥‥トシのコトは‥‥
English: I'm not... Mature...

Japanese: ‥‥私の考えは決まりました。
English: I've come to a conclusion...

Japanese: この証人が目撃したのは、 ９９パーセント、被告です。
English: I'm 99% certain that this witness saw the defendant.

Japanese: しかし！　１パーセントの ギモンの余地がある！
English: However! My remaining one percent of doubt is quite reasonable!

Japanese: ‥‥そこで。証人にもう一度だけ、 証言してもらいます。
English: Which means that for my peace of mind, I'm going to request a bit more testimony.

Japanese: な‥‥なんですって！
English: W-What!?

Japanese: その証言にムジュンがなければ ‥‥判決をくだしましょう。
English: If there are no contradictions in his next statement, I am prepared to issue a ruling.
(A ruling...!!)

Japanese: なるほどくん！ さ、最後のチャンスだよ‥‥！
English: Nick! This is your last chance!

Japanese: ～目撃した“人影”について・２～
English: -- The Silhouette, Part 2 --

Japanese: まちがいない！　ヤッパリ、 白いバラはつけてませんでした！
English: There's no doubt in my mind! There were no white roses that night!

Japanese: でも、他のシンボルに関しては まちがいないと思います。
English: However, all of the other symbols were there... I'm equally sure of that.

Japanese: 特にシルクハットのかざりには、 見おぼえがありました！
English: Especially the silk hat! There is no way I could forget seeing the decorations on it!

Japanese: 現場から立ち去るまで、 ちゃんとかぶってましたよ！
English: He was wearing it the entire time that he was on the scene!

Japanese: ‥‥弁護人。あなたに、一度だけ チャンスを与えます。
English: Mr. Wright... You've got one last chance at this.

Japanese: 一度だけ‥‥ですか？
English: Just one chance?

Japanese: 証人をゆさぶることは 許可しません。
English: I will not allow even the slightest hint of badgering against this witness.

Japanese: “この証人の見たことに、 ムジュンはあるのか？”
English: If you are going to prove to me there's a contradiction with Mr. Curls' statement,

Japanese: ‥‥たった１つの証拠品で、 それを証明するのです。
English: you'd better have at least a shred of evidence to back up your accusations.

Japanese: （チャンスは一度‥‥ ゆさぶることはできない‥‥）
English: (I've only got a single shot as this... I've got to be careful.)

Japanese: わかりました。 ‥‥それでじゅうぶんです！
English: I understand, Your Honor. One chance is all I will need!

Japanese: ～目撃した“人影”について・２～
English: -- The Silhouette, Part 2 --

Japanese: まちがいない！　ヤッパリ、 白いバラはつけてませんでした！
English: There's no doubt in my mind! There were no white roses that night!

Japanese: でも、他のシンボルに関しては まちがいないと思います。
English: However, all of the other symbols were there... I'm equally sure of that.

Japanese: 特にシルクハットのかざりには、 見おぼえがありました！
English: Especially the silk hat! There is no way I could forget seeing the decorations on it!

Japanese: 現場から立ち去るまで、 ちゃんとかぶってましたよ！
English: He was wearing it the entire time that he was on the scene!

Japanese: ど、どう？ なるほどくん‥‥。
English: Whaddya think, Nick?

Japanese: ここまで来て、 負けるわけにはいかないよ。
English: There's no way I'm going to lose after coming this far.

Japanese: 答えは、かならずある。 ‥‥法廷記録の中に！
English: There's gotta be an answer. And it's probably in the Court Record!

Japanese: トミーさん！ あなたは本当に‥‥
English: Moe... Be honest...

Japanese: うわっ！
English: OWWW!!

Japanese: ‥‥“ゆさぶり”は無用。 ヤクソクよ、成歩堂　龍一。
English: You promised Mr. Phoenix Wright. No more badgering!

Japanese: とびきりハデなペナルティを 食らいなさい！
English: I hope you enjoy this penalty!

Japanese: ううう‥‥しまった‥‥
English: Ungh... I'm finished...

Japanese: あ、あの。 ペナルティは、私が‥‥
English: Umm... Excuse me. I'm the one handing out the pen--

Japanese: かはあっ！
English: OWWW!!

Japanese: ‥‥証人。 証言をつづけて。
English: Witness, continue with your testimony.

Japanese: あの。オレっちには ‥‥ムチは？
English: You're not going to whip me too, are you?

Japanese: 法廷が終わったら、 好きなだけ食らわしてあげる。
English: Maybe after court... If you're into that kinda thing.

Japanese: 終わってからじゃ イミないんだよなあ‥‥
English: But isn't it kinda pointless if you only whip me after court?

Japanese: そのシルクハットって ‥‥これですか？
English: This wouldn't happen to be the silk hat you saw that night, would it?

Japanese: そう！　そいつだよ！ あいつがかぶっていたのは！
English: Yep! That's it! That's the hat he was wearing that night!

Japanese: まちがいありませんか？
English: No question in your mind?

Japanese: ソイツを見まちがうほうが どうかしてるでしょう！
English: Exactly how would one mistake a thing like that!?

Japanese: そうですか‥‥。
English: I see...

Japanese: な、何か問題でも？
English: Is there some sort of problem, Mr. Wright?

Japanese: 狩魔検事。
English: Ms. von Karma.

Japanese: このシルクハットは、 どこで見つかったんでしたっけ？
English: Where exactly was the silk hat found?

Japanese: 聞くまでもない！　殺人現場よ。
English: Must you always ask these questions!? It was found at the crime scene!!

Japanese: げ、現場‥‥ですって‥‥？
English: The c-c-crime scene... That means...

Japanese: シルクハットは 殺人現場に落ちていた！
English: The silk hat fell off at the crime scene!

Japanese: しかし、証人！ あなたはハッキリ証言した！
English: However the witness clearly testified to the contrary!!

Japanese: “犯人は、立ち去るときも シルクハットをかぶっていた”
English: The witness stated that "He was wearing it the entire time that he was on the scene!"

Japanese: う‥‥ウソおおおおおおおおおッ！
English: NoooooOOOOOooooo!! That's not trrrrrruuuuueeee!!

Japanese: 静粛に！　静粛に！　静粛に！ ‥‥コラ、証人！
English: ORDER! ORDER!! OOORRRDER!! MR. CURLS!!

Japanese: は、ハイッ！
English: Y-Yes, Your Honor?

Japanese: アナタ、なんですかさっきから！
English: WHAT IS THE MEANING OF ALL OF THIS!?

Japanese: いいトシして、イイカゲンなコト ばっかり！
English: You are old enough to know better than to behave like this in court!

Japanese: い‥‥いえ、その‥‥。 おっかしいなあ。
English: Hey! That's just not right!! That's so harsh!

Japanese: オカシイのはアナタの目、 記憶力、その他モロモロです！
English: What's not right here is your eyesight and your memory, amongst other things!!

Japanese: な‥‥なんでぇ！　なんでぇッ！ みんなでオレっちをイジメてさあ！
English: Wh... Why are you being so mean to me!! What did I do!?

Japanese: そりゃまあ、オレっちのギャグは チョイつまらねえかもしれねえよ？
English: Lemme guess... You just didn't like my jokes or something, right?

Japanese: でもなあ！　オレっちの記憶力と 目は、タカよりもすげぇっつうの！
English: You didn't have to go and insult my eyesight or my memory!? They're both great!

Japanese: なんなんだよ！　高いトコ座って 人をジイさんみたいに言うなよな！
English: Seriously!! Why!? Just because you are sitting above me doesn't mean you belong there!

Japanese: 少なくともオレっち、アンタより ずううっと若いんだあああああっ！
English: And no matter how old I get, I'll always be younger you!! WAAAAAHHHHHHHH!! *sobs*

Japanese: コラアァッ！　証人！ なな、なんてコトを‥‥！
English: Enough of these childish outbursts, Mr. Curls! Who do you think you are?

Japanese: だって！　オレっち、見たモン！ あれはマックスだったモン！
English: I saw him! I swear I saw him!! It was Max!!

Japanese: ムネにバラなんかつけてなかったし シルクハットもかぶってたモン！
English: Even if he didn't have his roses, he was still wearing his dumb silk hat!!

Japanese: ホントだモン！
English: I'm telling the truth!!

Japanese: ‥‥だだっ子みたいに なっちゃった。
English: He's turned into a bratty little kid.

Japanese: つくづくブキミな光景だなあ。
English: It's pitiful, isn't it?

Japanese: シルクハットをかぶったまま ‥‥現場を立ち去ったんだモン！
English: He left the scene wearing that dumb silk hat! He was there!!

Japanese: （現場を‥‥立ち去った？）
English: (He... Left the scene?)

Japanese: どうしたの？　なるほどくん。
English: What's the matter, Nick?

Japanese: ‥‥なんかさっきから、 気になることがあるんだ。
English: There's something I've been mulling over for a while now.

Japanese: ‥‥証人？
English: Moe?

Japanese: なんだよ！
English: Whaddya want!?

Japanese: さっきも“現場を立ち去った”と 証言しましたけど‥‥
English: You just said that he left the scene...

Japanese: 犯人は、どうやって 立ち去ったんですか？
English: Exactly how did the murderer leave the scene of the crime?

Japanese: えええッ！ い、いや‥‥そりゃ‥‥その。
English: WHAT!? He... Umm... He... Went...

Japanese: ど、“どうやって”と 言われてもさあ。‥‥なあ？
English: Whaddya mean "how did he leave the scene"? You can't ask me that!!

Japanese: 今は、証人の見た人物について ギロンをしてるのよ！
English: Mr. Phoenix Wright is badgering the witness, Your Honor!!

Japanese: しかし、証言はムジュンだらけで お話になりません！
English: This witness' testimony is so full of holes, Ms. von Karma's protest is useless!

Japanese: むむうぅ‥‥
English: Grrrr...

Japanese: ‥‥そうですね。この際、 証人のハナシを聞きましょう。
English: You've got a point. Let's hear what the witness has to say on this matter.

Japanese: ‥‥ハイ、いいでちゅか ピエロさん？
English: Is that alright with you, little guy?

Japanese: コ‥‥子供あつかい すんなよな！
English: Don't talk to me like I'm a little baby!!

Japanese: た‥‥立ち去るのに、 “どうやって”もないだろ！
English: Besides, what kind of stupid question is "how did he leave the crime scene!?"

Japanese: もちろん、その。フツーに 歩いて帰っていったんだよ！
English: The answer's obvious! He just turned around and walked away!!

Japanese: ‥‥果たして そうだったのでしょうか？
English: That's what I expected you'd say... You sure that's how it happened?

Japanese: ‥‥へっぷ！
English: Hmph!!

Japanese: ？　‥‥どうも、 リカイに苦しむのですが‥‥。
English: Huh? I'm not sure I know exactly where you're going with this...

Japanese: 弁護人は、さっきからミョーな トコロにコダワリますね。
English: Lawyers nowadays sure do love to harp on the smallest things.

Japanese: “犯人が現場を立ち去らなかった” という証拠でも、あるのですか？
English: Do you have any proof to counter his story as to how the criminal left the scene?

Japanese: さあ、どうですか！
English: How about this!?

Japanese: それは、あなたなりの ユーモアのつもりですか？
English: This isn't your idea of a joke is it, Mr. Wright?

Japanese: 笑えない。
English: Not very amusing, Mr. Phoenix Wright.

Japanese: それじゃカネは取れねえな。
English: You're not going to get paid for that one.

Japanese: ‥‥ハズレみたいだね。
English: It doesn't look like it worked...

Japanese: （なぜだ？　自分がトミーさんに なった気がするぞ‥‥）
English: (What happened? It's like I turned into Moe or something...)

Japanese: どうも私には リカイできないのですが‥‥
English: You'll have to excuse me, but I don't understand what you're getting at.

Japanese: この写真を見てください。
English: Look at this picture.

Japanese: ‥‥問題は、雪の上に残された 足跡です。
English: The problem is the footprints in the snow...

Japanese: あしあと‥‥？
English: Footprints?

Japanese: 写真には、たしかに 被害者の足跡は写っています！
English: In this photo, we can clearly see the footprints of the victim!

Japanese: ‥‥ところが 犯人の足跡がないッ！
English: However, where are the criminal's footprints? They aren't there!!

Japanese: ヤバババババッ！
English: Ai-yai-yai-yai-yai!!

Japanese: ‥‥さあ、トミーさん。犯人は どうやって立ち去ったんですか？
English: So Moe, exactly how did the criminal escape the scene?

Japanese: ぎゅ‥‥ぎゅむむう‥‥
English: Umm... He... Uh...

Japanese: 裁判長！
English: Your Honor!

Japanese: もはや、この証人の証言は、 カンゼンにアテになりません！
English: This witness has already proven that his testimony is completely unreliable!

Japanese: この証人ごと、すべての証言を 記録から消してしまうべきです！
English: I move to strike all of this witness' testimony from the court record!

Japanese: ‥‥そうですねえ。
English: I agree...

Japanese: たしかにこのピエロは、さっきから テキトーなコトばっかり‥‥
English: This clown's testimony is as rickety as the clown car he came to court in.

Japanese: チョット、待てえええッ！
English: WAIT JUST A SECOND!!

Japanese: どいつもこいつも オレをムシしやがって！
English: You guys can't just ignore everything I've said!!

Japanese: ああ、いいさ！　話してやるさ！ ホントのコトを‥‥
English: Fine fine! I'll tell you the truth this time...

Japanese: 待ちなさい！
English: YOU WAIT A SECOND!

Japanese: ‥‥よけいな発言は 私がユルさない！
English: I think you've said more than enough for today!!

Japanese: イタくないッ！
English: That didn't hurt!!

Japanese: ‥‥もう、アンタの言うことなんか 聞かないモン！
English: I'm sick and tired of listening to you anyways!!

Japanese: オレっち、ブチまけちゃうんだあ！ ホントのコトをッ！
English: I'll give you the real deal this time... I swear!!

Japanese: な‥‥なんか、また タイヘンなコトになってきたよ‥。
English: I dunno why, but I get the feeling things are gonna get worse before they get better.

Japanese: トミーさん。
English: Mr. Lawrence Curls.

Japanese: お‥‥おうッ！
English: Yes!?

Japanese: あなたは‥‥今まで、 ウソの証言をしていたのですか？
English: The testimony you've provided up until now has been false?

Japanese: なな、なんでだよ！ そうじゃねえって。ただ‥‥
English: It hasn't been false! I haven't lied! It's just...
It's just what?

Japanese: “犯人が立ち去った”ってのは‥‥ チョットちがうんだ。
English: It's just I was a bit confused on the bit about the criminal leaving the scene.

Japanese: そこのムチの検事さんが “証言するな”って！
English: Especially since Von Karma and her whip told me not to talk about what I really saw!

Japanese: 静粛に！　静粛に！ ‥‥静まりなさいッ！
English: ORDER!! ORDER!! I WILL HAVE ORDER!!

Japanese: かかか、狩魔検事！ あああ、あなたはッ！
English: Franziska von Karma!! How could you!!

Japanese: 裁判長‥‥。
English: Your Honor...

Japanese: もし、この証人の言う “ホントのコト”を聞いたら‥‥、
English: If you had heard the truth from this witness...

Japanese: きっとアナタも、 私と同じ意見を持つはず。
English: You would have exactly the same opinion as I have.

Japanese: な‥‥なんですか？
English: What opinion is that?

Japanese: “笑えない”‥‥と。
English: It's not funny!

Japanese: もうよろしい！ とにかく、話を聞くのです。
English: That's enough out of you! I'm going to listen to what he has to say.

Japanese: ‥‥さあ！　あなたの見た “ホントのコト”を証言しなさい！
English: Now then, let's hear the truth about what you say you saw!

Japanese: ヘッ！ 聞いておどろけよな！
English: Hah! You're not gonna believe this but it's all true I tell you!

Japanese: しかし、笑わないように。
English: Try not to waste our time with your idiotic drivel.

Japanese: ～“ホントのコト”～
English: -- The Truth --

Japanese: さあ、“トミーさんは見た！” のコーナーでぇす！
English: Now it's time for our next segment, "Moe Knows..."!!

Japanese: 今までの証言は、みーんな ホントのことだったんだ！
English: Everything that I've said up until now has been the truth!

Japanese: オレっちが見たとき、団長が 倒れていて、マックスが立ってた！
English: When I looked out the window, the Ringmaster was down and Max was standing above him!

Japanese: 白いバラはつけてなかったし、 シルクハットをかぶってたんだ！
English: He wasn't wearing his white roses, but he was wearing the silk hat!

Japanese: それで‥‥オレっちが見ていたら ‥‥あ、アイツは‥‥
English: That's when I saw... He...

Japanese: ‥‥これが真相なんだあ！
English: This is the truth now, get ready for it!

Japanese: アイツは‥‥飛んだんだ！ 空に向かって、ふわああ‥‥って！
English: He flew! He jumped up and flew through the air!!

Japanese: ‥‥そのまま、ヤミの中へ 消えッちまったんだあ！
English: He flew right off and disappeared into the darkness!

Japanese: 足跡なんて、残るわけねえさ！ ‥‥だって、飛んだんだもん！
English: That's why there were no footprints! Flying people don't leave footprints!

Japanese: ‥‥以上。
English: I told you it wasn't funny. Do you believe me now?

Japanese: あ‥‥その。 な、なんと言いますか‥‥
English: Well... That was... Umm... How do you put this into words...

Japanese: ‥‥マックス・ギャラクティカは 世界的な大魔術師。
English: Maximillion Galactica is a world-class magician.

Japanese: しかし。現場から飛び去った‥‥ そんなことは、あり得ない。
English: But to leave the scene of a crime by flying. There's no way that actually happened.

Japanese: そ‥‥そうですな‥‥。
English: You... You're right.

Japanese: なんでだよ！　他の証言は 信じてくれるのに！
English: Why is she right!? You believed the other witnesses!

Japanese: なんで、一番オイシイ部分は 信じてくれないんだよおお！
English: Why won't you believe me!? Especially since it's the best part of the story!!

Japanese: ‥‥ふ、ふむう‥‥私も さすがに、こんなの初めてです。
English: Hmm... To be honest, this is the first time I've heard of a flying criminal.

Japanese: この証人の目撃‥‥。 どう思いますか？　弁護人。
English: What do you think about this witness' testimony, Mr. Wright?

Japanese: ‥‥さ、さすがに、証人の 見まちがいでは‥‥
English: It's obvious that the witness' eyes are playing tricks on him...

Japanese: バ、バカ言うない！
English: Don't be such a fool!

Japanese: あのヤローと、いったいナニを 見まちがえるって言うんだよ！
English: Who in the world do you think I would mistake that jerk for!?

Japanese: （ウソをついている ワケじゃない‥‥）
English: (Jeez... I didn't say you were lying!)

Japanese: ‥‥っていうか、今までで イチバン、マジメなカオしてるよ！
English: Wow, he looks really serious right now. More serious than he's looked all day!

Japanese: ‥‥ここまでムチャなコトを、 冗談で言うとは思えません。
English: What he just said was so strange, I don't think he would have made it up.

Japanese: ということは‥‥証人は、 真実を述べているのだ、と？
English: Which means that he is telling the truth?

Japanese: そ‥‥そうですねえ‥‥
English: That's what I think...

Japanese: ま、待ってよなるほどくん！
English: Nick, wait!

Japanese: それじゃあ、マックスが魔術を 使ったってコトになっちゃう！
English: That means that Max actually used magic!!

Japanese: しまった！　そうなるな‥‥。
English: Yikes! You're right!

Japanese: あいたッ！
English: OWWW!!

Japanese: バカが見るバカなユメに バカなユメを見るバカ‥‥。
English: Only a foolish looking fool could be fooled by such a foolish fool's foolish dream.

Japanese: 魔術はゲンジツではない。 ‥‥まやかしなのよ！
English: Don't be ridiculous! Magic does not exist!

Japanese: ‥‥正直なところ、 信じられません。
English: Right now, I can't believe any of this!

Japanese: 今日のこの法廷は、 ユメだったんじゃないかと‥‥
English: I'm not even sure if this day hasn't been some kind of Kafkaesque dream...

Japanese: あいてッ！
English: OWWW!!

Japanese: ‥‥あなたがユメを見て どうするの！　成歩堂　龍一ッ！
English: Now do you think it's still a dream!? Huh, Mr. Phoenix Wright!?

Japanese: ‥‥それでは、私の 考えを述べたいと思います。
English: I suppose I will let you all in on my thinking regarding this matter...

Japanese: “犯人が空に消えた” たしかに、信じがたいハナシです。
English: "The criminal disappeared into the sky." I'd love to believe that...

Japanese: しかし、これをアタマから ムシすることはできません。
English: But I just can't wrap my head around how that could actually happen.

Japanese: バ、バカな‥‥！
English: You imbecile!

Japanese: この証言を証拠と考えない 狩魔検事のタイドは、リッパです。
English: If you disregard a need for proof, Ms. von Karma's case is sound.
However...

Japanese: どうやら、この件は、もう少し 調査が必要なようですな。
English: I've got the feeling that this case is in dire need of more investigation.

Japanese: 本日の審理は、 ここまでとします！
English: Thus I will conclude today's proceedings at this point!

Japanese: 現場に犯人の足跡がないのは、 まちがいのないジジツです。
English: It's an undisputed fact that the criminal left no footprints at the scene.

Japanese: ‥‥明日までに、このナゾの 答えを探してくるように。
English: Tomorrow, I want us to find out the reason behind this mystery of mysteries.

Japanese: は、はあ‥‥。
English: Umm, uhh...

Japanese: ‥‥うむむ‥‥
English: Grrr...

Japanese: それでは、 本日は、これにて閉廷！
English: I believe that's enough for today. Court is now in recess!

Japanese: 同日　午後　２時３３分 地方裁判所　被告人第５控え室
English: December 29, 2:33 PM District Court Defendant Lobby No. 5

Japanese: お、おい‥‥ハニイたち！ いったい、どーなっているんだい！
English: Hey! Sweeties! What in the world is going on!?

Japanese: こっちが聞きたいですよ！
English: That's what I want to know!

Japanese: ハンニンが、 空を飛んで消えた‥‥なんて。
English: They say the criminal flew off into the air and disappeared!

Japanese: マックスさん。　ホントに、 飛んでないんでしょうね‥‥？
English: Max, I can't believe I'm asking this, but you didn't fly that night, did you?

Japanese: ババ、バ、バカ言わないで くれたまえよ！
English: I know you didn't mean to ask me such a fabulously stupid question!

Japanese: そんなカンタンに 飛べるワケないだろう！
English: I can't fly whenever I please. It's not that easy!!

Japanese: でも、ステージでは、 ずいぶんキモチよさそうに‥‥
English: But it looks so effortless for you on stage...

Japanese: カンベンしてけれェェッ！ オラにそんな、できるワケねえだ！
English: It's not that simple!! I'm not actually flying on stage!

Japanese: 見えないヒモさ使って つるしてるだけなんだあああ！
English: I use invisible wires and have them hoist me through the air!

Japanese: ‥‥ショ‥‥ショーゲキの コクハク‥‥なのかな‥‥
English: Wow... You just told me the secret to your magic!!

Japanese: 魔術なんて‥‥魔術なんて‥‥ オラは‥‥
English: NO!! I broke the first rule... The cardinal rule... The ONLY RULE!

Japanese: ‥‥す、すみません。
English: I'm sorry, Max.

Japanese: あまりのことに、 ついカッとなっちゃって‥‥。
English: I'm sorry you had to break a magician's creed to keep the magic tricks secret...

Japanese: なるほどくん‥‥。
English: Nick! What do we do now!?

Japanese: ‥‥探すしかないな、明日までに。 空に消えたハンニンを。
English: All we can do now is hope we find the flying criminal in court tomorrow.

Japanese: そうだね！ うまくつかまるといいケド。
English: Great idea! Let's do our best and catch this sucker!

Japanese: つづく
English: To be continued.

Japanese: そこまで！
English: That's enough!

Japanese: 本法廷は、これ以上の審議の 必要性を認めません。
English: This court sees no reason to further prolong this trial.

Japanese: さらに、被告に対する判決を 決める時間も、必要としません。
English: Nor is there any need for more time to decide the case against the defendant.

Japanese: これは、きわめて明白な事件です。 疑問の余地はない！
English: This case is extremely clear. I see no room for mis- interpretation of the facts.

Japanese: 被告人、山田　耕平に この場で、判決を言いわたします！
English: This court finds the defendant, Mr. Billy Bob Johns...

Japanese: 被告人はこれより身柄を 司直に預けるものとし、
English: The accused will surrender to the court immediately,

Japanese: １ヶ月以内に、高等裁判所において 通常裁判を受けることとします。
English: to be held pending trial at a higher court within a month from today's date.

Japanese: では、本日はこれにて閉廷！
English: That is all. The court is adjourned!
